patterns_recognized: Stage 1/2 disagreement (f5_full_delta >= 0.12) has resolved in Stage 2/full_prob's direction across essentially every tracked case since 7/9 (LAA@MIN, SEA@TB, NYY@WSH, NYM@PHI on 7/16) -- never treat a large delta as an OVERRIDE signal toward Stage 1's lean; today's PIT@CLE (delta=0.27) and DET@LAA (delta=0.32) both fit this pattern.

D-grade picks with both SP-xERA and PQI opposing the pick split close to 50/50 directionally across the archive (e.g. 4W/3L on 7/11) -- SKIP is the correct response even on today's most lopsided cases (WSH@OAK, DET@LAA); fading to the opposing team is not supported by the data.

HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD/DIAMOND) remains the single most validated cap in the archive, correctly calling directional losses on elite pre-cap stacks on multiple days (7/9, 7/10, 7/11) -- never relax it, including on TEX@ATL today.

The HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket 'CONFIRM rescue' to full pre-cap tier is only validated when CAP 10 is the sole blocker and all four dimensions (F-signal, SP-xERA, PQI, Stage-agree) affirm at pre-cap score>=5 (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA) -- it does not extend to games where HARD CAP 7 (bullpen disadvantage) also fires, since CAP 7 has its own separate ~7-for-8 validation record. MIA@MIL today hits both caps simultaneously despite a perfect pre-cap score=10 stack.

Today's slate is unusually thin -- every game is SKIP-tier or fully capped to non-bettable; the only two nominal PLATINUM/GOLD tiers (SD@KC, MIA@MIL) both collapse under HARD CAP 10, and MIA@MIL additionally triggers HARD CAP 7. Two starters (TB's Sandoval, ATL's Murphy) are also gated by the thin_sp_data HARD_VETO (<100 Statcast pitches).

PIT @ CLE.reasoning: Edge is a thin +2.46pp (outside the [4,15]pp actionable band) and HARD CAP 10 correctly zeroed the bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.513. Stage 1 gave CLE a big F5 lean (75.7%) but Stage 2/full_prob flipped to PIT (48.7% CLE, delta=0.27) -- per the 7/9-7/16 archive, Stage 2 direction has been the more reliable read on large deltas, so this is not an override signal. SP edge and PQI both agree with PIT but the pick stays correctly unstaked.

MIN @ CHC.reasoning: Edge is -8.89pp (negative, outside band). Both SP xERA (Taj Bradley favored over Boyd by 1.08) and PQI (against CHC) oppose the pick -- a classic D-grade dual-opposing-signal configuration. The archive shows this signature splits close to 50/50 directionally (4W/3L on 7/11 alone), so SKIP is correct and no override to MIN is warranted.

CHW @ TOR.reasoning: PQI confirms TOR (Δ=+8.5) and Stage 1/2 agree (Δ=0.03), a cleaner stack than most SKIPs today, but HARD CAP 10 zeroed it at pick_prob=0.530 (bottom-bucket, no CONFIRM rescue since pre-cap score was only 2/B). Edge (+4.73pp) sits inside the normally-actionable band, so this is a probability-bucket suppression rather than a signal-quality problem. Confirming the cap.

CIN @ COL.reasoning: Edge +1.59pp is negligible. SP xERA (Rhett Lowder over Sugano, 1.59 gap) and PQI (Δ=-9.8) both oppose the COL pick, and Stage 1/2 agree so there's no disagreement nuance to weigh. Same D-grade dual-opposing pattern as MIN@CHC -- SKIP is correct, no override.

NYM @ PHI.reasoning: Model picks NYM even though its own SP (Manaea) trails Luzardo (PHI) by 1.26 xERA, PQI runs Δ=+25.4 against NYM, and Vegas's fair_prob (0.373) is far below the model's implied confidence -- edge +16.46pp is large but stays below the +25pp CAP 6 hallucination line. This shape (big edge, fair_prob<0.42, SP+PQI both against) is close to what HARD CAP 6 targets; the existing D grade already reflects appropriate skepticism. CONFIRM SKIP, no stake either side.

BAL @ HOU.reasoning: HOU (home) is picked despite Trevor Rogers (BAL) holding a 0.93 xERA edge over Arrighetti and PQI running Δ=-12.6 against HOU. Stage 1/2 agree, so there's no disagreement complicating the read -- just a straightforward dual-opposing D-grade. Consistent with the archive's guidance to SKIP and not fade to BAL on this signature alone.

SD @ KC.reasoning: The nominal PLATINUM tier is misleading: the only supporting signal is F3_swing_take_gap=613.5, well below the ~1000+ magnitude the archive treats as meaningful, with no SP-xERA or PQI corroboration (pre-cap score only 2/B). HARD CAP 10 correctly zeroed the bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.519. Combined with the standing PLATINUM-skepticism heuristic (~43% historical hit rate), there's no case for elevating out of SKIP.

STL @ ARI.reasoning: Dustin May (STL) holds a 0.68 xERA edge over Pfaadt and PQI runs Δ=-6.5 against the ARI pick; Stage 1/2 agree. Another clean dual-opposing D-grade -- SKIP correct, no fade to STL per the archived rule against overriding this signature.

TB @ BOS.reasoning: Patrick Sandoval (TB) has thrown only 65 Statcast pitches this season (need 100+), triggering the sp_savant_gate HARD_VETO -- exactly the rookie/thin-sample SP blindspot the heuristics flag. HARD CAP 10 also zeroes the residual bottom-bucket score (pick_prob=0.519, pre-cap only 1/B-). CONFIRM SKIP -- no reliable edge exists until Sandoval clears the sample threshold.

TEX @ ATL.reasoning: Owen Murphy (ATL, the picked team's own starter) has just 67 Statcast pitches -- the same thin-sample HARD_VETO as TB@BOS, meaning ATL's own SP-quality signal is unreliable. Despite a decent pre-cap B+ stack (F-signal, PQI confirms Δ=+5.2, Stage agree, score=3), HARD CAP 1 (-0.36pp edge) and HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.511) stack to zero it. CONFIRM SKIP; the rookie-SP uncertainty on ATL's own side reinforces the caution.

MIA @ MIL.reasoning: MIL's pre-cap stack is the best on the slate (score=10/A): F-signal, SP edge agrees (+2), PQI confirms (Δ=+7.2), Stage 1/2 agree -- the exact four-dimension-affirming profile that justified the twice-validated HARD CAP 10 rescue (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA). But CAP 10 isn't the only blocker here: HARD CAP 7 (bullpen disadvantage, gap=+0.0185) also fires, and that cap has its own separate ~7-for-8 validation record distinct from the CAP-10-only rescue precedent. The lone CAP-7 miss (KC@BAL, +0.018 gap) was a near-identical sub-threshold magnitude, so there's a live question about whether this gap size is meaningful -- but not enough evidence to override two stacked caps at once on a single day's stack. CONFIRM SKIP; do not extend the CAP-10-only rescue exception to a game where CAP 7 also fires.

SF @ SEA.reasoning: Bryan Woo (SEA) holds a 0.45 xERA edge over Webb and PQI runs against the SF pick (Δ=+7.1); Stage 1/2 agree. Dual-opposing D-grade, same pattern as the other SKIPs today -- SKIP correct, no override.

LAD @ NYY.reasoning: The CSV still labels this GOLD, but the post-cap grade is D (score=-1): Emmet Sheehan (LAD) holds a 0.51 xERA edge over Weathers, PQI runs Δ=-11.0 against NYY, and only F5_bullpen_gap=+0.98 supports the pick. No hard cap needed to fire -- ordinary scoring already collapsed this to non-bettable. Treat the GOLD label as stale; CONFIRM the effective SKIP.

WSH @ OAK.reasoning: This is the slate's most lopsided opposing stack: Zack Littell (OAK) holds a 2.32 xERA edge over Ginn, PQI runs Δ=+34.8 against WSH (the largest opposing PQI today), and the model's +16.08pp edge implies far more confidence than fair_prob (0.468) supports. This looks like a fade candidate, but per the 7/9 archive rule, D-grade dual-opposing picks should be SKIPped, not overridden to the other side -- the historical split (4W/3L) means directional certainty isn't there even with unusually strong opposing signals. CONFIRM SKIP.

DET @ LAA.reasoning: Worst grade on the slate (D, score=-4): Tarik Skubal (DET) carries a massive 3.78 xERA edge over Rodriguez, PQI runs Δ=-38.3 against LAA (largest on the slate), team_quality opposes, and Stage 1/2 disagree (Δ=0.14). Four-plus signals opposing simultaneously matches the archived 'signal-opposition = noise' pattern (see 7/8 LAA@TEX) -- treat the LAA direction itself as unreliable, not just low-tier. Already fully SKIP, so CONFIRM; no stake is at risk either way.

PIT @ CLE (G2).reasoning: Doubleheader nightcap; PIT's (away) probable SP is still unannounced both in the diag CSV and on live statsapi as of this run (CLE's home SP, Logan Allen, is confirmed). No pick is possible until PIT names its starter. CONFIRM the pending status.
